qa_pairs.question: How does the Alliance charge research groups for core equivalents when jobs request varying amounts of cores and memory on shared cluster resources?
qa_pairs.answer: The Alliance charges research groups for the maximum number of core equivalents they utilize from the resources. Assuming a core equivalent is 1 core and 4GB of memory, if a job requests more cores than memory (above this 1 core/4GB ratio), it will be charged based on the number of cores. If it requests more memory than this ratio, it will be charged based on the memory requested, with the job request being counted as the higher of these two equivalent amounts when priority is calculated.

qa_pairs.question: What is the principle for how research groups are charged for Reference GPU Unit (RGU) equivalents, including associated cores and memory, by the scheduler on Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: When using GPU-based allocations, which include a Reference GPU Unit (RGU) alongside multiple cores and memory in a bundle, research groups are charged for the maximum number of RGU-core-memory bundles they use. This means if a job requests more RGUs, cores, or memory relative to the defined proportions of a fictive RGU-core-memory bundle, the charge will be calculated based on the component (RGU, cores, or memory) that represents the highest number of these equivalent bundles.
